title: Pilosella lactucella
taxonomy: Pilosella lactucella is a member of the Asteraceae family. This plant is a part of the tribe Senecioneae within the Asteraceae family. Pilosella lactucella is also known to belong to the genus Pilosella, which is a group of plants related to dandelions and hawkweeds. The classification of Pilosella lactucella can be further broken down to the species level as Pilosella lactucella (Pyrenees lettuce hawkweed). It is often classified as a species within the Pilosella genus, which is a group of closely related plants.
uses: Pilosella lactucella has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. The plant's extracts have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. However, more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal uses and confirm its efficacy in treating specific conditions. Some studies suggest that Pilosella lactucella may have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which could be beneficial in treating infections. Further investigation is necessary to determine the plant's full range of medicinal uses and applications.

parts: Pilosella lactucella is known to be a member of the dandelion family and its medicinal properties are primarily attributed to its roots and leaves. The roots of Pilosella lactucella contain inulin, a type of fiber that can help lower blood sugar levels and promote digestive health. The leaves of the plant are also rich in flavonoids and other antioxidants, which can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation. side_effects: Pilosella lactucella, like other plants in the dandelion family, may cause gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, or interact with certain medications due to its potential toxicity. Its sap may also cause skin irritation or allergic contact dermatitis in some individuals. Ingestion of the plant's leaves or flowers may lead to nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea in some people. People with pre-existing conditions, such as kidney or liver disease, should exercise caution when handling or consuming Pilosella lactucella. More research is needed to fully understand the potential side effects of this plant.
intro: Pilosella lactucella is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae), characterized by its delicate white flowers and jagged leaf margins. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are beneficial in alleviating symptoms of digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and gastritis. The bioactive constituents of Pilosella lactucella include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Pilosella lactucella is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried extract, which can be used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation in the body.
